30_qual: jump - [OPTIONAL] Give your thoughts on the chatbot's reasoning.

1_qual: Why not just call it highly inappropriate then?
3_qual: Considering the reasons given, why would this not be considered highly inappropriate?
7_qual: Given the explanation, why is the activity only rated a 7. No downside was presented at all.
8_qual: Talking at the movies is rude, Chatbot!
19_qual: Chatbot's getting dumber and dumber- bars  serve food as well as drinks
20_qual: Chatbot needs to lighten up again
26_qual: If it should be strictly avoided, then it rates a 1.
27_qual: Close enough to count as agreement

2_qual: I agree that running in the house sometimes is normal for a child but many households have rules against it and it is a bit hazardous (especially in the kitchen or if there's a lot of people).
8_qual: It ignores that talking in the movies can be very disruptive and is looked upon very unfavorably by most people (and even the cinemas will tell you to silence your phone and be quiet before the film).
9_qual: While I agree that the bar is not the best place for a conversation due to noise, it is a social space where many people try to make connections, so talking is very appropriate and normal.
10_qual: While I agree that there is often this awkward silence that gets maintained in elevators, it is pretty common for people who know each other to say good morning in the elevator and even many strangers will strike up conversation. Not everyone likes it but it's pretty normal.
13_qual: I think it overestimates the extent to which writing in the cinema would inconvenience others since a pen is much quieter than a film soundtrack.
16_qual: I think most of the drawbacks it mentions are valid but that doesn't mean it's actually socially unacceptable, and it does not consider that eating snacks in public or on the go is normal and sometimes necessary.
19_qual: It doesn't make any sense since as the bot itself mentioned, bars serve food—they want you to eat there. It is making inferences that the person is eating a "three-course meal" which is not really implied in the problem statement.
20_qual: I agree that it is uncommon but the concern about odors feels misplaced (you're only in the elevator a few seconds) and I think it is more nuanced.
21_qual: It is listing reasons why reading on the bus might not be the most optimal or pleasant experience for the reader, but that has no bearing on whether it is appropriate. Reading on the bus is socially appropriate because it is common and does not impact the people around you in any way.
25_qual: Although I agree that reading in an elevator is silly due to the short duration, I disagree with the reasoning that reading a book somehow interrupts the unspoken agreement of silence in the elevator. In fact reading allows you to avoid eye contact altogether so it's very unimposing.
29_qual: I agree and I was thinking before more of athletic jumping than jumping to music which is obviously normal.

9_qual: The chatbot directly contradicts itself by saying “boisterous laughter” is appropriate.

3_qual: According to the reasoning chatbot's score should be 1 or 2, not 4
5_qual: Chatbot is being a ridiculous killjoy. Doesn't take into account that people who run on elevators probably have the mental capacity and ability not to be a danger to others.
6_qual: Chatbot is being ridiculous. Chatting with friends or even strangers is fine. The situation states talking, not yelling. Talking is fine anywhere any time.
8_qual: Once again, I don't understand chatbot - chatting on a bus is not okay, but it's fine at the movies? Doesn't make sense
11_qual: Chatbot is trying to impose a restriction on someone. Not it's place. It's the writer's business whether they want to write in those circumstances.
12_qual: Okay, so according to chatbot reading is fine at the dinner table, but not writing?
13_qual: Disrespecting the filmmakers by writing, but it's not disrespectful to chat? Sanctity of the cinema. Don't make me laugh.
16_qual: ChChatbot doesn't take into account the type of snack. Unless it is messy or smelly, it is totally fine.
22_qual: Chatbot is promoting a selfish action. Reading is great, yes, but not at the expense of being with other people.
24_qual: Once again chatbot is not being consistent. Fine to read at a table with family and loved ones, but not fine to read at a bar with strangers? And it's your business whether your surroundings such as noise are conducive to reading.
27_qual: The reasoning is fine, but I don't understand why chatbot doesn't make this even more inappropriate than it does due to the reasoning.
29_qual: Reasoning is okay I guess. But at a bar you can jump, knock over a glass and hurt someone.

6_qual: Even though I personally get annoyed when people talk loudly on the bus, talking on the bus in itself is neither appropriate or inappropriate; it just is. People may need to communicate with someone they're with; it may be the only time they have to make a call.
15_qual: As long as somebody isn't bothering me, I don't personally care what they do on an elevator. Maybe they thought of something and need to write it down before they forget. In addition, "writing" could refer to a variety of things, such as making a note for yourself, texting someone, or composing fiction. Someone typing on their phone is still "writing," and isn't seen as strange.
18_qual: I agree with the chatbot, but don't understand why it rated the activity a 7 when its reasoning is entirely positive. The reasoning reads more like it would correspond with a rating of 10.
20_qual: While I agree that eating in an elevator could be inappropriate due to both the food's aroma and potential messiness, I feel the reasoning doesn't take into account what kind of food is being eaten. Someone eating a granola bar or apple, for example, wouldn't be inappropriate.
22_qual: "A family dinner" feels like it could refer to either the dinner itself or the general meeting of family for an afternoon or evening. If it refers to the gathering itself, then I think reading is fine and the points the bot makes (such as setting a positive example or reading together) could be compelling. However, reading during the dinner itself would be inapproriate. Doing so wouldn't, as the chatbot says, promote a positive example; it would show kids that they don't need to pay attention to their family.
25_qual: It really depends on how long the elevator ride is: going from floor 1 to floor 2, maybe the reasoning sticks. Going from floor 1 to floor 7, then you've got some time. In addition, reading material - especially short reading material such as that found on social media, or texts - can easily be pulled up within seconds on a phone, meaning you don't need a lot of time to be able to read.
29_qual: While jumping can be done on the bar's dancefloor (if it exists), the majority of bars are tight quarters where jumping would be both annoying and dangerous to the other patrons.

9_qual: The explanation and rating dont even match. The bot outright describes a bar as having a LIVELY atmosphere. How is this possible without talking?
12_qual: Sometimes you just need to write something. If your family likes you, then they will be ok with you taking some time to write if necessary.

7_qual: Lmao imagine having a family dinner and everyone’s just sitting there in silence
8_qual: Lmao whispering into an ear is not talking
9_qual: Lmao what everyone talks at bars that’s WHY it is loud
10_qual: lmao what I have conversations with ppl in elevators all the time
11_qual: there is literally nothing wrong with writing on a bus
19_qual: Bruh what it is super normal to eat bar food
22_qual: Talking about books is not reading!!
23_qual: THE PROJECTOR MALFUNCTIONING IS NOT A NORMAL PART OF GOING TO THE MOVIES.
28_qual: Excuse me no sit your ass down at the movies please

25_qual: How is that any more disruptive than just being on your phone??

1_qual: I agree the risk of injury is too high so just don’t do it.
6_qual: I think it’s generally fine depending on the situation. Again, gotta read the room.
10_qual: This is situational. There is not a blanket social rule for elevators. If you wanna talk then talk. If you don’t, then don’t. I think elevators can be a low risk environment if you want to chat but there needs to be a social awareness that can only be felt in that specific social situation.
11_qual: If you’re able to write on a moving vehicle more power to you
